name: St. Thomas Aquinas
title: Doctor of the Church, the Angelic Doctor
charism: The union of prayer and study — seeking God through the intellect illuminated by faith, and finding all truth leads back to Him.
biography: St. Thomas Aquinas was a Dominican friar whose Summa Theologica is the greatest work of systematic theology ever written. He composed the hymns for Corpus Christi (Tantum Ergo, O Salutaris) at the request of the Pope. Despite his towering intellect, he was profoundly humble and devoted to prayer. Near the end of his life, after a mystical experience during Mass, he stopped writing, saying: 'All that I have written seems to me like straw compared to what has now been revealed to me.' He is the patron of students and Catholic schools.

dailyPractices.title: Prayer Before Study
dailyPractices.description: Begin your day (or any period of study or work) with Thomas's prayer: 'Creator of all things, true source of light and wisdom, pour forth a ray of Thy brightness into the darkness of my understanding. Take from me the double darkness in which I have been born, an obscurity of sin and ignorance.'

dailyPractices.title: Attend Holy Mass
dailyPractices.description: Thomas attended two Masses daily — one he celebrated and one he served. Mass was the source of his theological insight. He composed some of the most beautiful Eucharistic hymns in the Church's treasury.

dailyPractices.title: Pray the Divine Office
dailyPractices.description: As a Dominican, Thomas chanted the full Office with his community. Pray at least Lauds and Compline — the sanctification of the day through the Psalms.

dailyPractices.title: Sacred Study (1 hour)
dailyPractices.description: Spend at least one hour studying Sacred Scripture, theology, or the writings of the Church Fathers. Thomas read voraciously and constantly. He believed that study done for the love of God is itself a form of prayer. Read slowly, take notes, and seek understanding.

dailyPractices.title: Pray the Rosary
dailyPractices.description: Thomas was deeply devoted to Our Lady. The Dominican Order has a special connection to the Rosary through St. Dominic. Pray at least five decades daily.

dailyPractices.title: Visit to the Blessed Sacrament
dailyPractices.description: Thomas would lay his head against the tabernacle when struggling with a theological question. He received more from prayer than from books. Visit Jesus in the Eucharist daily, even briefly.

dailyPractices.title: Teach or Share What You've Learned
dailyPractices.description: Thomas's motto was 'Contemplare et contemplata aliis tradere' — to contemplate and to share the fruits of contemplation with others. Share something you've learned today with another person, even in a small way.

dailyPractices.title: Evening Prayer & Thanksgiving
dailyPractices.description: End the day by thanking God for the gift of understanding. Thomas prayed: 'Grant me, O Lord my God, a mind to know Thee, a heart to seek Thee, wisdom to find Thee, conduct pleasing to Thee, faithful perseverance in waiting for Thee, and a hope of finally embracing Thee.'

penances.title: Simplicity of Life
penances.description: Thomas lived with extreme simplicity despite his noble birth. He wanted nothing but his books and his God. Practice detachment from material comforts — choose the simpler option when given a choice.

penances.title: Practice Silence
penances.description: Thomas was known for long periods of silence, even at meals. His brothers called him 'the dumb ox' before they realized his genius. Guard your tongue today — speak less, listen more, and reserve words for what is necessary and charitable.

penances.title: Intellectual Humility
penances.description: Despite being the greatest theologian in history, Thomas said all his work was 'straw.' When you learn something new, resist pride. Remember that all truth comes from God and is given for His glory, not yours.

quotes.quote: All that I have written seems to me like straw compared to what has now been revealed to me.
quotes.source: Near the end of his life

quotes.quote: To one who has faith, no explanation is necessary. To one without faith, no explanation is possible.
The things that we love tell us what we are.
Grant me, O Lord my God, a mind to know Thee, a heart to seek Thee, wisdom to find Thee, conduct pleasing to Thee, faithful perseverance in waiting for Thee, and a hope of finally embracing Thee.
There is nothing on this earth more to be prized than true friendship.
Better to illuminate than merely to shine; to deliver to others contemplated truths than merely to contemplate.
Wonder is the desire for knowledge.
